steps.title: Find the slope of line r
steps.content: Rearrange $5x - 2y = 10$ into slope-intercept form: $$-2y = -5x + 10 \implies y = \frac{5}{2}x - 5$$ Slope of line $r$ $= \frac{5}{2}$.

steps.title: Test all four choices for the slope of a parallel line
steps.content:
• Choice A — -5: Slope $= -5 eq rac{5}{2}$. Not parallel. ✗
  • Choice B — -2: Slope $= -2 eq rac{5}{2}$. Not parallel. ✗
  • Choice C — $ rac{2}{5}$: Slope $= rac{2}{5} eq rac{5}{2}$. Not parallel (this is the reciprocal, not the same slope). ✗
  • Choice D — $ rac{5}{2}$: Slope $= rac{5}{2}$. Matches slope of line $r$ exactly. Parallel lines have equal slopes. ✓

steps.title: Conclusion
steps.content: Parallel lines have the same slope. Line $r$ has slope $\frac{5}{2}$, so line $s$ must also have slope $\frac{5}{2}$. That confirms D is correct.
